source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The thin lens shown in Figure has surface radii of 15.00 cm (left) and 10.00 cm (right). The refractive index of the glass is known to be 1.5. Calculate the focal length of this lens.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure'
the text gives left/right radii magnitudes but not the full lens shape; the figure is likely needed to infer surface orientation/signs
multi_step: solving requires combining visual interpretation of the lens shape with the lensmaker formula
the task involves at least two steps: determine radius signs/orientation from the figure, then compute focal length from n and the two radii
multiple-choice format with a single numeric result suggests a short but nontrivial calculation
verifiability: answer is a single numeric quantity ('60 cm') with clear units
choices are provided, enabling straightforward result checking against the computed value
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and the physics formula application is externally checkable

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are available
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high verifiability score because the answer is a single numeric value with units and can be checked directly
the problem is image-dependent, but the required figure is available, so the main recovery risk is mitigated
multi-step visual-plus-formula reasoning makes it a useful candidate despite some sign-convention sensitivity
